requested_by: david (via dark-factory production engine proof run)
prompt:
Update the dark-factory constellation map to add the 4 micro-apps born 2026-07-03. Work in this repo (dark-factory), from its root.

STEP 1 — read the schema. Open mochaccino/data/constellation.json and look at the existing entries in its "apps" array (fields: id, name, layer, path, purpose, status, status_detail, port, build_target, verdict_notes) and its "flows" array (fields: from, to, type, basis, note). Follow that exact shape for the new entries — do not invent new fields.

STEP 2 — add 4 new entries to the "apps" array, one per app below. For each, pick the best-fitting "layer" from {data-source, data-plane, surface-core} based on what the app actually does (read apps/<name>/README.md if you want to confirm), pick "status" from {running, on-demand} based on whether it has a launchd daemon/pulse or is invoked on demand, and set build_target to "kbde-extension" for all four (they are all extension-track micro-apps per dark-factory convention). Use each app's real path (apps/<name>) and a short accurate purpose string.

  - omi-fetch (apps/omi-fetch): always-on OMI wearable transcript pulse (launchd, every 600s) — fetches new OMI conversations, archives verbatim to raw-intake, indexes them, emits an event for transcripts with >=3 segments. Dark-factory's first extension-track micro-app.
  - app-registry (apps/app-registry): reads mochaccino/data/constellation.json (structure) plus fresh live process/port probes on this machine (liveness) every 1800s via launchd, and writes a combined registry that flags drift when the two disagree.
  - project-digest (apps/project-digest): read-on-demand (no daemon) per-project digest generator; reads durable sources fresh each run and writes exactly one piece of state, store/last-briefing/<project-id>.json.
  - kdd-viewer (apps/kdd-viewer): read-on-demand (no daemon), read-only viewer over any project's docs/kdd/ knowledge store (learnings, patterns, decisions/ADRs, examples) with a rendered HTML view.

STEP 3 — add matching entries to the "flows" array: one inferred data-feed edge per new app from a real upstream source, following the exact style of the existing app-specific entries (e.g. "appyradar-sentinel" -> "appyradar", type "data-feed", basis "inferred", with a one-line note). Suggested edges (adjust if you find a better-supported one while reading the READMEs, your judgment call, note why in the flow's "note" field):
  - omi-fetch -> cortex (it produces raw transcript data useful to the brain store, same rationale as the existing omi-ingester -> cortex edge)
  - inventory-registries -> app-registry (app-registry explicitly reads constellation.json / the registries as its structure source)
  - app-registry -> project-digest (project-digest's briefing draws on registry state)
  - project-git-mission-state -> kdd-viewer (kdd-viewer scans each project's docs/kdd/ folder, which lives under project-git-mission-state)

STEP 4 — update mochaccino/designs/10-constellation/index.html: find the <script type="application/json" id="constellation-data"> block and replace its JSON content with the FULL updated constellation.json content (the embedded copy must match the file byte-for-byte in terms of app ids/fields — this is a rendered gallery page, the embedded copy is what actually renders).

STEP 5 — commit locally in THIS repo only (dark-factory). Do NOT push. Stage only the two files you changed (mochaccino/data/constellation.json and mochaccino/designs/10-constellation/index.html) and commit with EXACTLY this message:
feat(constellation): add the 2026-07-03 micro-apps (via factory engine)

STEP 6 — write your status report as instructed in the turn that gave you this ticket (a results/<ticket>.json file) with the commit sha you produced, then ACK.
